metadata.credit: Data and analysis provided by Draftsim
metadata.author: Bryan Hohns (Veveil)

set_overview.summary: Wilds of Eldraine is a fast but balanced format with several viable playstyles and decks. The format features a rock-paper-scissors dynamic: Rx aggro beats Ux control, Golgari Food counters aggro, and control beats Food.

color_notes.{R} Red: Best color. Excellent removal, efficient creatures, three common Treasure-makers enabling splashes.
color_notes.{B} Black: Best common (Candy Grapple), solid curve filler, strong bargain setup.
color_notes.{G} Green: Strong ramp/fixing spells and fatties, but lacks removal.
color_notes.{W} White: Sweet combat tricks (Archon's Glory), good cheap creatures.
color_notes.{U} Blue: Strongest card-draw spells but struggles creating independent board presence.

key_gameplay_notes: Bargain spells exceed baseline expectations — prioritize enablers like Food tokens and enchantments.
Red is strongest (removal + creatures), blue is weakest (needs support to matter).
Even Selesnya Roles succeeds on curve before role synergies — build mana curves first.
Most decks play 17 lands; Simic plays 18; Boros may trim to 15-16.
Three common Treasure-makers enable splashes; green fixing via Rootrider Faun and Brave the Wilds.

mechanics.name: Bargain
mechanics.summary: Pay an alternative cost by sacrificing a resource (enchantment, Food, etc.) when casting the spell.
mechanics.performance: Overperforms baseline expectations. Enablers (Food tokens, cheap enchantments) are premium.

mechanics.name: Food Tokens
mechanics.summary: Artifact tokens you can sacrifice for 3 life. Created by Golgari payoffs; fuel for sacrifice synergies and Greta activation.

mechanics.name: Celebration
mechanics.summary: Triggers when you cast two or more spells of different colors in a turn.
mechanics.performance: Easy to trigger in Boros/Rakdos; payoffs are solid creatures.

mechanics.name: Roles
mechanics.summary: Enchantment tokens (Cursed, Young Hero, Royal, Sorcerer, Wicked, Virtuous, Monster) placed on creatures.
mechanics.performance: Selesnya-focused; flexible utility for combat tricks elsewhere.

mechanics.name: Adventures
mechanics.summary: Split-card spells playable in two modes — Adventure (instant/sorcery) or creature side.
mechanics.performance: Universal value; Johann, Apprentice Sorcerer enables recurring use.

mechanics.name: Treasure Tokens
mechanics.summary: Artifact mana sources enabling color fixing. Created by several red commons.

mechanics.name: Tapping Effects
mechanics.summary: Azorius Frost theme (Icewrought Sentry, Hylda cards). Underpowered — payoffs depend on rares.

tier_1.name: Golgari Food (BG)
tier_1.strategy: Midrange with removal + curve filler + fatties; minimal flier density.

tier_1.name: Boros Aggro (RW)
tier_1.strategy: Fast with strong redundancy in creature slots. Many creatures at 1-4 CMC; triggers celebration easily.

tier_1.name: Rakdos Rats (BR)
tier_1.strategy: Midrange/aggro creating wide boards. Synergizes with Enchanting Tales bonus sheet cards (Goblin Bombardment, Shared Animosity). Particularly strong against Simic.

tier_2.name: Gruul Monsters (RG)
tier_2.strategy: Straightforward curve emphasizing large creatures (4/x theme rarely matters).

key_cards.name: Ferocious Werefox
key_cards.type_line: Creature — Elf Fox Warrior // Instant — Adventure

tier_2.name: Selesnya Roles (GW)
tier_2.strategy: Enchantment-focused. Role creatures excel; Curse generates value.

key_cards.name: Ferocious Werefox
key_cards.type_line: Creature — Elf Fox Warrior // Instant — Adventure

tier_2.name: Dimir Faeries (UB)
tier_2.strategy: Tempo deck with cheap fliers, removal, and countermagic. Ideal build: cheap curve, heavy interaction, two Into the Fae Court at top.

tier_3.name: Orzhov Midrange (WB)
tier_3.strategy: Grindy long-game with incremental card/board advantage. Leverages bargain spells; enchantment-to-graveyard triggers Neva.

tier_3.name: Izzet Spells (UR)
tier_3.strategy: Controlling. Sits behind Unruly Catapult and Johann to build incremental advantage. Lacks sweepers; Treasure-makers enable splashes.

tier_3.name: Simic Ramp (GU)
tier_3.strategy: Mana-hungry ramp/control. Outclassed by Golgari. Most demanding mana count (17-18 lands).

tier_4.name: Azorius Frost (WU)
tier_4.strategy: Only truly bad archetype. Depends on rare payoffs. 60% win rate when payoffs drawn, 49% without them.

{G} Green.name: Ferocious Werefox
{G} Green.type_line: Creature — Elf Fox Warrior // Instant — Adventure

matchup_specific.name: Ice Out
matchup_specific.note: Best vs Hamlet Glutton decks; worst vs Boros/Rats.

matchup_specific.name: Spider Food
matchup_specific.note: Sideboard vs fliers or broken rares (Virtue of Loyalty).

general_tips: Prioritize signpost cards (Johann, Candy Grapple, Greta, Ash) — they reveal archetype directions.
Bargain overperforms; prioritize enablers like Food tokens and cheap enchantments.
Build mana curves first; theme synergies are secondary.
Avoid Azorius Frost unless Icewrought Sentry and tapping payoffs flow your way.
In BO3, side out mediocre creatures vs fast decks; side in removal/greedy cards based on matchup.
Aggro decks race (Boros, Rakdos); midrange balances (Golgari, Gruul); control grinds (Izzet, Dimir).
